remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Progressive neurodegeneration — no spontaneous remission; untreated motor decline averages 3-5 UPDRS points/year; non-motor symptoms (depression, cognitive decline, autonomic dysfunction) also progressive
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Levodopa/carbidopa: 40-50% motor improvement in early disease but efficacy wanes (motor fluctuations in 50% at 5 years, dyskinesias in 30%); deep brain stimulation (DBS): 50-60% reduction in off-time, 60-70% reduction in dyskinesias at 5 years; LCIG (levodopa-carbidopa intestinal gel infusion): near-continuous on-time in advanced patients
remission.gap_size: Large — DBS significantly underused; no disease-modifying therapy exists; current treatments are purely symptomatic
remission.barrier_detail: No neuroprotective or disease-modifying treatment is approved despite decades of research; DBS is offered too late in disease course (often 10+ years after diagnosis vs evidence for benefit in earlier disease — EARLYSTIM trial); focused ultrasound (FDA cleared 2021) underused as non-invasive alternative
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — FDA cleared focused ultrasound thalamotomy (FUS) for bilateral PD tremor; 2021 FDA cleared Parkinson's-specific wearable (KinetiGraph); levodopa has been the backbone since 1967 — one of the longest-unchanged standards in medicine
remission.outdated_treatments: Anticholinergic drugs (benztropine, trihexyphenidyl) for tremor in older patients (severe cognitive and urinary side effects; inappropriate in >65 years yet still prescribed); amantadine at standard doses for dyskinesia before considering DBS; MAO-B inhibitors without reassessment of levodopa optimisation
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Exercise (SPARX and CYCLE trials: high-intensity exercise slows motor progression; potentially neuroprotective); focused ultrasound (FUS) thalamotomy; dietary: gut-brain axis interventions (prebiotics, probiotics — emerging Phase 2 data); photobiomodulation for neuroprotection (early trials)
remission.frontier_signal: Alpha-synuclein immunotherapy (prasinezumab, PASADENA/PADOVA trials — Phase 2 equivocal for motor, positive on certain imaging biomarkers); GLP-1 agonists (lixisenatide PPMI Phase 2 — significant motor benefit signal 2024); gene therapy (AAV2-AADC for advanced PD; AAV-GDNF Phase 2); stem cell-derived dopaminergic neurons (Aspen Neuroscience, BlueRock — Phase 1/2)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
